title: Cat Panting Like a Dog: Is it an Emergency?
meta_desc: Is your cat panting like a dog? Open-mouth breathing in cats is a high-priority emergency. Learn why and what to do now.
content_html:
What Does It Mean When a Cat Pants?

While dogs pant to cool down after exercise, it is very rare for a healthy cat to breathe with an open mouth. When a cat pants like a dog, it is typically a sign of severe respiratory distress or an underlying cardiovascular issue. This behavior indicates that your cat is working much harder than normal to move oxygen into their lungs and bloodstream.

Is Cat Panting an Emergency?

Yes. The urgency level for a panting cat is High. Open-mouth breathing is considered a critical medical emergency in the feline world. If your cat is currently panting, you should contact the nearest emergency veterinary clinic immediately. Do not wait for an appointment; time is of the essence to provide oxygen therapy and stabilize their condition.

How Photos and Videos Help Triage

Providing a short video of your cat’s breathing can be an invaluable tool for the veterinary triage team. A video allows the vet to assess the respiratory rate, the effort of the abdominal muscles, and the posture of the cat. This visual information helps the clinic prepare the necessary life-support equipment, such as an oxygen cage, before you even arrive at the hospital.

faqs.question: Could my cat just be hot or stressed?
faqs.answer: While extreme heat or intense fear can occasionally cause brief panting, it is much more likely to be a sign of a serious medical condition like heart failure, asthma, or fluid in the chest. Because the risk is so high, any cat panting like a dog should be evaluated by a vet immediately.

faqs.question: What should I do while transporting my cat to the vet?
faqs.answer: Keep the car cool and quiet. Minimize stress by handling your cat as gently as possible. If they are struggling to breathe, do not try to restrain them or force them into a position that makes them uncomfortable.

faqs.question: What are other signs of breathing trouble in cats?
faqs.answer: Other red flags include breathing with the elbows pointed outward, blue or pale gums, extreme lethargy, or using the stomach muscles to push air out.
